sections.paragraph:
ἀσβόλη, ης, ἡ (Semonides 7, 61 Diehl; Diosc. 5, 161 W.; La 4:8 for ἡ ἄσβολος; Galen XVI 623, 8 ὑπὸ τ. πολλῶν ἀσβόλην, ἣν ἄσβολον οἱ Ἕλληνες. Cp. Lob., Phryn. p. 113) soot, typical of blackness Hs 9, 1, 5; 9, 6, 4.—DELG s.v. ἄσβολος.

Diehl Diehl , ed., s. AnthLG—List 5

Diosc Diosc , or Dioscurides, I a.d. physician, also Ps.-Dioscorides after I a.d.—List 5

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

Lob. Lob. = CLobeck, s. Phryn.—List 5

Phryn Phryn , the Atticist, signifying his Eclogae, II a.d.—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 141
